reviewerView.question: Should "Sell Specialty" be marked as misplaced under "Sell (Physical Object)"?
reviewerView.currentState: Sell Specialty is currently under Sell (Physical Object) in the Physical Object branch.
reviewerView.proposedState: "Sell Specialty" does not belong under "Sell (Physical Object)".
reviewerView.reasoning: A specialty is a category, expertise, or featured offering and is not necessarily a physical object that can be sold. A specialty is not inherently a kind of physical object; it can be an area of expertise, service, or specialized offering, so selling a specialty is not always selling a physical object. H6: other — A specialty is not inherently a kind of physical object; it can be an area of expertise, service, or specialized offering, so selling a specialty is not always selling a physical object.

reviewerView.question: Should "Sell Service (1)" be marked as misplaced under "Sell (Information)"?
reviewerView.currentState: Sell Service (1) is currently under Sell (Information) in the Information branch.
reviewerView.reasoning: A service is labor or an activity arrangement rather than an informational record, even if it is described or contracted through documents. Sell Service (1) transfers a service, which is fundamentally an act, labor, or ongoing arrangement provided to the recipient, so the Actors and Activities branch applies. H1: belongs in Actors and Activities (high) — Sell Service (1) transfers a service, which is fundamentally an act, labor, or ongoing arrangement provided to the recipient, so the Actors and Activities branch applies.

reviewerView.question: Should "Sell Ticket" be marked as misplaced under "Sell (Physical Object)"?
reviewerView.currentState: Sell Ticket is currently under Sell (Physical Object) in the Physical Object branch.
reviewerView.proposedState: "Sell Ticket" does not belong under "Sell (Physical Object)".
reviewerView.reasoning: A ticket’s value is primarily as a record of entitlement or access, not as the physical paper or token itself. Sell Ticket transfers access to an entertainment, travel, or event activity rather than a tangible object or pure recorded content, so the service/access branch applies. H1: belongs in Actors and Activities (high) — Sell Ticket transfers access to an entertainment, travel, or event activity rather than a tangible object or pure recorded content, so the service/access branch applies.
